refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-006 (Refined): The NGHE solution shall generate and maintain a real-time worksite model representing terrain elevation, static obstacles (rocks, structures, vegetation), dynamic hazards (personnel, other equipment), and designated work zones with sufficient fidelity to support autonomous path planning, collision avoidance, and operator situational awareness.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on scope (what constitutes 'area analysis') and lacked explicit linkage to downstream functional consumers (planning, execution, safety). Refinement adds: (1) real-time update cadence to support dynamic safety; (2) explicit feature taxonomy (terrain, static obstacles, dynamic hazards, work zones); (3) fidelity requirement tied to use cases (path planning, collision avoidance, situational awareness); (4) availability requirement for planning/execution functions per acceptance criteria.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Acceptance test: Worksite model is generated within 5 minutes of system initialization, updated at ≥2 Hz for dynamic features, contains ≥95% of pre-surveyed obstacles within ±0.5 m horizontal accuracy, and is accessible via standardized query interface to planning and safety modules.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain and obstacle data from onboard and/or external sensors.
CR-NGHE-006.2: Fuse multi-source sensor data into a unified spatial model.
CR-NGHE-006.3: Classify and label worksite features (terrain, obstacles, hazards, work zones).
CR-NGHE-006.4: Maintain and update the worksite model in real-time with dynamic feature tracking.
CR-NGHE-006.5: Expose worksite model via standardized interface to planning, execution, and safety functions.
CR-NGHE-006.6: Enforce access control and data integrity for worksite model queries.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized mapping service (SLAM/sensor fusion engine) running on vehicle compute, publishing model updates via message broker to planning and safety subscribers.
DP-B: Distributed sensor processing with local feature extraction on each sensor node, aggregated by a central coordinator into a unified model.
DP-C: Cloud-based worksite modeling service receiving telemetry from vehicle and external sensors, computing model, and pushing updates back to vehicle and operator consoles.
DP-D: Hybrid approach: onboard real-time local model (for safety-critical collision avoidance) + cloud-based detailed model (for planning and analytics).
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid onboard/cloud worksite modeling with real-time local SLAM/sensor fusion on vehicle for safety-critical functions and cloud-based detailed model for planning, analytics, and operator situational awareness. Onboard model updates at ≥2 Hz; cloud model updates at ≥1 Hz with latency tolerance of 2–5 seconds for non-safety functions.

potential_sources: Coupling between onboard model and cloud model: requires bidirectional synchronization and conflict resolution. Mitigation: onboard model is authoritative for safety-critical decisions; cloud model is advisory for planning.
Coupling between worksite model and sensor acquisition (CR-NGHE-006.1): model fidelity depends on sensor quality and coverage. Mitigation: explicit sensor requirements and fallback modes.
Coupling between worksite model and planning/execution functions: model schema and update frequency must match consumer expectations. Mitigation: standardized interface contract (IM layer).
coupling_check.notes: DP-D decouples safety-critical real-time requirements (onboard) from planning/analytics requirements (cloud), reducing coupling complexity. Hybrid approach is justified by high safety criticality and need for operator situational awareness.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Onboard compute has sufficient capacity for real-time SLAM/sensor fusion (GPU or specialized accelerator available).
Assumption: Network link to cloud is reliable enough for asynchronous model updates; loss of cloud connectivity does not degrade safety.
Risk: Onboard/cloud model divergence could lead to operator confusion or planning errors. Mitigation: explicit sync protocol and conflict resolution rules.
Risk: Sensor coverage gaps (e.g., occluded areas, sensor blind spots) may result in incomplete model. Mitigation: explicit coverage analysis and operator alerts.
Risk: Real-time SLAM performance degrades in GPS-denied or featureless environments. Mitigation: multi-sensor fusion (LiDAR, camera, IMU, wheel odometry) and fallback to dead reckoning.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify onboard compute architecture (dedicated SLAM processor vs. shared vehicle compute) and define explicit model synchronization protocol (timestamps, versioning, conflict resolution) before IM assignment.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Onboard real-time worksite modeling subsystem: sensor acquisition (LiDAR, camera, IMU), local SLAM/sensor fusion engine, real-time model storage, and model query interface for onboard safety and planning functions. Excludes cloud-based detailed modeling and operator console visualization (assigned to Teleoperation and Safety Services).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle publishes real-time worksite model updates (terrain grid, obstacle list, dynamic hazard positions, work zone boundaries) and sensor quality metrics to cloud and operator consoles at ≥2 Hz.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives work zone definitions, mission-specific feature labels, and model update priorities from planning/execution functions.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle publishes collision risk alerts and model confidence warnings to Safety Services when obstacles or hazards are detected within safety thresholds.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle exchanges model updates, sensor telemetry, and synchronization messages with cloud-based modeling service and operator consoles.

module_containment_scores.notes: Vehicle is the natural home for onboard real-time SLAM/sensor fusion (high cohesion with sensor acquisition and safety-critical collision avoidance). Diffusion risk: model query interface must be standardized to avoid tight coupling with planning/execution modules. Mitigation: define IM-level API contract.

Teleoperation could host cloud-based detailed modeling and operator visualization, but onboard real-time model should remain on Vehicle for safety criticality and latency. Diffusion risk: splitting model logic across Vehicle and Teleoperation increases synchronization complexity.

Safety Services could consume worksite model for hazard detection and risk assessment, but should not own model generation (violates single responsibility). Diffusion risk: if Safety Services also generates/updates model, creates circular dependency with Vehicle.
NGHE Solution is too coarse-grained to own worksite modeling; would diffuse responsibility across multiple subsystems. Not recommended.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: Vehicle (primary: onboard real-time model); Teleoperation (secondary: cloud-based detailed model and operator visualization).
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Onboard worksite modeling (sensor fusion, real-time model, safety-critical queries) is tightly coupled to Vehicle's sensor suite and safety functions, justifying containment in Vehicle. Cloud-based detailed modeling and operator visualization are decoupled from Vehicle's real-time constraints and can be hosted in Teleoperation or a separate cloud service module (not yet defined in available modules).
containment_risks: Risk: Vehicle module may become overloaded if onboard compute is shared with other functions (e.g., autonomous driving, teleoperation control). Mitigation: allocate dedicated compute resource (GPU/SLAM processor) and define priority scheduling.

Risk: Worksite model query interface may become a bottleneck if many onboard functions (planning, safety, diagnostics) access it concurrently. Mitigation: implement efficient spatial indexing and caching.

Risk: Cloud-based detailed model is not assigned to any available module (Teleoperation is a candidate but not explicitly scoped). Mitigation: clarify whether cloud modeling is in scope for this decomposition level; if yes, recommend creating a dedicated 'Cloud Services' or 'Mapping Service' module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define cloud-based modeling as a separate IM (e.g., 'Cloud Mapping Service') or assign it to Teleoperation with clear interface contracts. Define onboard/cloud model synchronization protocol and conflict resolution rules at IM level.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP-D (hybrid onboard/cloud modeling) directly addresses refined FR requirements: (1) real-time model generation via onboard SLAM; (2) feature taxonomy support (terrain, obstacles, hazards, work zones) via sensor fusion; (3) fidelity tied to use cases (path planning, collision avoidance, situational awareness); (4) availability to planning/execution via standardized interface. DP-D is justified by high safety criticality (onboard real-time model) and operational needs (cloud-based planning support). No independence violations detected.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: Onboard/cloud model synchronization logic may be split between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, creating tight coupling. Mitigation: define explicit sync protocol at IM boundary (e.g., message schema, versioning, conflict resolution).

Diffusion risk: Worksite model query interface may be implemented differently by Vehicle and Teleoperation, leading to inconsistency. Mitigation: standardize API contract (e.g., REST, gRPC, or message-based) at IM level.
Diffusion risk: Sensor acquisition and fusion logic may leak into planning/execution modules if model interface is not well-defined. Mitigation: enforce strict IM boundary with documented API.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle containment score of 0.92 is strong for onboard real-time modeling. Cloud-based modeling is not well-contained in available modules (Teleoperation score 0.65); recommend clarifying scope or creating dedicated module. Onboard/cloud split is architecturally sound but requires explicit synchronization contract.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via integration testing: (1) sensor data injection tests to validate model accuracy; (2) latency tests to confirm ≥2 Hz update rate; (3) interface contract tests to verify query API; (4) safety tests to confirm collision avoidance alerts. Acceptance test criteria are measurable (accuracy ±0.5 m, update rate ≥2 Hz, feature coverage ≥95%).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Vehicle owns onboard real-time model; Teleoperation (or future Cloud Services module) owns cloud-based detailed model. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined. Ownership boundaries are explicit.

decision.justification: Refined FR-NGHE-006 is not atomic; it decomposes into six sub-FRs (sensor acquisition, data fusion, feature classification, real-time updates, model exposure, access control). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define concrete DPs and IMs. DP-D (hybrid onboard/cloud modeling) is sound but requires detailed design of synchronization protocol, model schema, and API contracts before implementation. IM assignment to Vehicle is justified, but cloud-based modeling requires clarification of scope and module ownership. Proceeding to next decomposition level is necessary to allocate sub-FRs to specific DPs and IMs.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain and obstacle data from onboard and/or external sensors.
CR-NGHE-006.2: Fuse multi-source sensor data into a unified spatial model.
CR-NGHE-006.3: Classify and label worksite features (terrain, obstacles, hazards, work zones).
CR-NGHE-006.4: Maintain and update the worksite model in real-time with dynamic feature tracking.
CR-NGHE-006.5: Expose worksite model via standardized interface to planning, execution, and safety functions.
CR-NGHE-006.6: Enforce access control and data integrity for worksite model queries.

assumptions: Onboard compute has sufficient capacity (GPU or SLAM accelerator) for real-time SLAM at ≥2 Hz.
Network link to cloud is reliable for asynchronous model updates; loss of connectivity does not degrade safety.
Sensor suite (LiDAR, camera, IMU) provides sufficient coverage and accuracy for ±0.5 m obstacle detection.
Planning and execution functions can tolerate 2–5 second latency for cloud-based model updates.
Worksite model schema is standardized and versioned to support onboard/cloud synchronization.
Safety-critical collision avoidance relies on onboard real-time model; cloud model is advisory only.
open_risks: Onboard/cloud model divergence could lead to operator confusion or planning errors. Requires explicit sync protocol and conflict resolution.
Sensor coverage gaps (occluded areas, blind spots) may result in incomplete model. Requires coverage analysis and operator alerts.
Real-time SLAM performance may degrade in GPS-denied or featureless environments. Requires multi-sensor fusion and fallback modes.
Cloud-based modeling is not assigned to any available module; scope and ownership are unclear.
Worksite model query interface may become a bottleneck if many onboard functions access it concurrently. Requires efficient spatial indexing and caching.
High safety criticality (CR-NGHE-006 is safety-critical) requires rigorous validation of model accuracy and update latency.
evidence_needed: Onboard compute architecture specification (processor, GPU, memory, power budget).
Sensor suite specification (LiDAR range/resolution, camera FOV, IMU accuracy, update rates).
SLAM algorithm selection and performance benchmarks (accuracy, latency, robustness in GPS-denied environments).
Worksite model schema definition (data types, coordinate systems, feature taxonomy, versioning).
Onboard/cloud synchronization protocol specification (message format, conflict resolution, retry logic).
Worksite model query API specification (REST, gRPC, or message-based; query types; latency SLAs).
Safety validation plan (model accuracy testing, collision avoidance testing, operator situational awareness testing).
Network link reliability analysis (bandwidth, latency, packet loss tolerance).
